name: MoneyGram
description_short: MoneyGram is a global money transfer service offering domestic and international funds transfers, bill payments, and cash services through thousands of agent locations.
description_long:
MoneyGram is an established money transfer and financial services company that operates through a network of agent locations rather than physical branches. The company enables consumers to send and receive money domestically and internationally, with services accessible both online and at physical locations. MoneyGram partners with various retail and service businesses—including pharmacies, supermarkets, wireless retailers, and specialized money service centers—to provide convenient access points for money transfers and related financial services across the United States and globally.

The company's core offerings include domestic and international money transfers, allowing customers to send funds to recipients and receive incoming transfers. MoneyGram services are available through multiple channels: customers can initiate transfers online through the MoneyGram website and mobile platform, or conduct transactions in person at agent locations. The platform enables flexible fund delivery options and supports both sending and receiving transactions. Customers can search for nearby agent locations by address, city, state, or ZIP code to access services conveniently.

MoneyGram's distinguishing factor is its extensive agent network rather than traditional banking infrastructure. By partnering with established retailers and service providers—including Walmart locations, pharmacies, wireless stores, and specialized money service businesses—MoneyGram provides widespread geographic coverage and accessibility. This model allows the company to serve consumers who may not have traditional bank accounts or prefer the convenience of established local merchants for financial transactions.

MoneyGram serves a legitimate financial need for money transfers and bill payment services, though consumers should be aware that transfer fees and exchange rates (for international transfers) apply. The service model is most valuable for those sending funds to recipients without traditional banking access or those preferring cash-based transactions. Customers should compare MoneyGram's fees against competitors like Western Union before sending larger amounts, as transfer costs can be significant depending on destination and amount.
pros: Extensive agent location network including Walmart, pharmacies, wireless retailers, and specialized money service centers for broad accessibility
Online and offline transaction options allowing customers to send/receive money through website or in-person at agent locations
Simple location search functionality by address, city, state, or ZIP code to find nearest service points
Available in Union City with multiple local agent locations open until evening hours for convenience
Global money transfer capability enabling both domestic and international funds transfers
No requirement for bank account to send or receive money transfers
Real-time or next-day funding options for domestic transfers depending on service selected
cons: Transfer fees apply to all transactions; costs can be significant for larger amounts and international transfers
Exchange rates for international transfers may not be competitive compared to banks or specialized remittance providers
Agent location hours vary by merchant partner; not all locations offer 24/7 service
Receiving money requires recipient to visit an agent location in cash rather than direct deposit option
Limited financial protection compared to bank deposits; funds not FDIC insured
best_for: Consumers sending money to recipients without traditional bank accounts who prefer cash pickup
Individuals needing to send urgent domestic or international funds without bank account requirements
Users preferring in-person cash-based transactions over digital banking platforms
People seeking convenient evening/weekend money transfer access through local retail merchants
diagnosis: MoneyGram is best for consumers who need to send domestic or international money transfers and lack traditional bank accounts or prefer cash-based transactions. The main caveat is that transfer fees and foreign exchange rates can be substantial—customers should compare costs with competitors before sending large amounts, and international recipients must visit agent locations to collect funds rather than receive direct deposits.
services: Domestic money transfers within the United States
International money transfers to multiple countries
Online money transfer initiation through website and mobile app
In-person money transfer at physical agent locations
Incoming money transfer receipt and cash pickup
Multiple currency support for international transfers
Agent location search and store finder by address/city/state/ZIP
Same-day or next-day transfer delivery options
